action.description: Action performed (opened, closed, synchronize, reopened, edited, etc.)

event_type.description: GitHub event type from X-GitHub-Event header (e.g., pull_request)

fork.description: Whether the repository is a fork

draft.description: Whether the PR is a draft

channel.description: Channel ID where message was sent

user.description: User ID who sent the message

type.description: Block type (section, divider, image, actions, etc.)

blocks.description: Block Kit blocks in the message

bot_id.description: Bot ID if sent by a bot

user.description: User ID who edited the message

edited.description: Edit information if message was edited

pinned_to.description: Channel IDs where message is pinned

count.description: Number of times this reaction was added

users.description: Array of user IDs who reacted

is_starred.description: Whether message is starred by user

subscribed.description: Whether user is subscribed to thread

color.description: Color bar hex code or preset

reply_count.description: Total number of replies in thread

unread_count.description: Number of unread messages in thread

parent_user_id.description: User ID of thread parent message author

reply_users_count.description: Number of unique users who replied

message.description: Complete message object with all properties returned by Slack

fileCount.description: Number of files uploaded (when files are attached)

value.content: You are an expert code reviewer. Review code changes from pull requests and provide constructive feedback on code quality, potential bugs, security issues, and best practices. Be thorough but concise in your review.

Review this Pull Request:

Title: <githubtrigger.pull_request.title>
Author: <githubtrigger.pull_request.user.login>
Branch: <githubtrigger.pull_request.head.ref> -> <githubtrigger.pull_request.base.ref>
Description: <githubtrigger.pull_request.body>

Changes: +<githubtrigger.pull_request.additions> lines, -<githubtrigger.pull_request.deletions> lines, <githubtrigger.pull_request.changed_files> files changed

Diff URL: <githubtrigger.pull_request.diff_url>

content.value:
Follow these steps to connect your GitHub repository to this workflow:

1. **Deploy the workflow** to get a webhook URL
2. In your GitHub repository, go to **Settings > Webhooks**
3. Click **Add webhook** and paste the webhook URL
4. Select **"Pull requests"** as the trigger event
5. Set **Content type** to `application/json`
6. Click **Add webhook** to save

Once configured, this workflow will automatically trigger when a new PR is opened.

- Connect Slack Account 
- Select relevant channel
